commit.message:
fix: startup unmute watcher killing daemon after 60s timeout

Two bugs in _startup_unmute_watcher introduced in smooth restart:
1. unmute_task was in asyncio.wait(FIRST_COMPLETED) — its completion
   after 60s timeout killed the daemon. Now fire-and-forget.
2. Unconditional unmute on timeout even when no audio streamed —
   now skips unmute if audio_streaming was never True.

commit.message:
Fix security vulnerabilities in auth endpoints (closes #13) (#15)

Security Fixes:
- Add open redirect prevention: Validate returnUrl is local path before redirecting
- Change logout from GET to POST: Prevents CSRF logout attacks
- Update LoginDisplay.razor: Use form with POST method and AntiforgeryToken

Implementation Details:
- Added IsLocalUrl() helper to validate URLs start with / but not //
- Rejects http://, https://, and protocol-relative URLs
- Logout endpoint now requires POST method
- Antiforgery validation via UseAntiforgery() middleware

Testing:
- Added Web.Tests project with integration tests
- Tests verify URL validation logic for open redirect protection
- Tests verify GET logout returns 404 (endpoint removed)
- Tests verify POST logout requires authentication
- All 12 tests passing

References:
- OWASP Open Redirect: https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Unvalidated_Redirects_and_Forwards_Cheat_Sheet.html

commit.message:
feat: smooth restart — mute sinks, cache sink names, auto-unmute

Three improvements for glitch-free service restarts:

1. Startup sink mute: daemon_process mutes PA sink before BridgeDaemon
   starts, _startup_unmute_watcher unmutes after audio_streaming=True
   + 1.5s stabilization delay (60s timeout safety)

2. Pre-restart local mute: saveAndRestart() mutes all local PA sinks
   via force_local before stopping — no MA pause/play needed, so sync
   group members on other bridges keep playing uninterrupted

3. Sink name cache: configure_bluetooth_audio() saves found sink name
   to LAST_SINKS[mac] in config.json; on restart tries cached name
   first, skipping 3s A2DP delay + retries if still valid

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas (#2)

* fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas

SummarizeRequest and SummarizeAgainRequest had `percentage: int` with
`ge=30, le=80`, which rejected `0` or `null` from the frontend with a
422 Unprocessable Entity.

Changed both to `int | None` with `ge=1, le=100` so callers can omit
or null-out the field; `_get_percentage()` already handles the
default fallback to a random 30-80 value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: widen percentage range to 1-100 across models and simplify fallback

- Text model: percentage ge=30,le=80 → ge=1,le=100
- Summary model: same range update
- _get_percentage: replace random 30-80 fallback with deterministic
  default of 50%; respect user's explicit choice via schema validation
- Remove unused `random` import

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add GitHub Actions workflow to auto-deploy to FastAPI Cloud

Triggers on every push to main. Installs fastapi-cli, restores the
auth token from the FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH GitHub secret, then runs
`fastapi deploy . --no-wait` which uses the app_id from .fastapicloud/cloud.json.

Setup required:
1. Copy the contents of ~/.fastapi/auth.json on your local machine
2. Add it as a GitHub secret named FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH in the repo settings

feat: store timezone on events instead of inferring from creator (#121)

Events now persist their timezone at creation time rather than looking up
the creator's profile timezone. This fixes issues where:
- Creator changes their profile timezone after creating an event
- Creator is deleted (was falling back to first attendee's timezone)
- Creator travels to a different timezone

Changes:
- Add timezone column (varchar, NOT NULL, default 'America/Los_Angeles')
  to events table with migration that backfills existing rows
- Save form-selected timezone when creating/editing events
- Reminder webhooks use event.timezone directly (removed leftJoin on
  users table that PR #119 added)
- Event edit route now shows timezone selector and uses event's stored
  timezone for time conversion
- InlineTimezoneSelector gains onChange callback prop
- Email notifications still format per-recipient timezone (unchanged)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
